findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "framefetch_platform_capabilities" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Return a JSON matrix of which data types (metadata, insights, transcript, frames) each supported platform provides — YouTube, YouTube Shorts, TikTok, Instagram …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: framefetch_extract, framefetch_platform_capabilities, framefetch_account · Private data: framefetch_extract · Public sink: framefetch_account.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: framefetch_extract, framefetch_platform_capabilities, framefetch_account · Private data: framefetch_extract · Public sink: framefetch_account

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
